criteria: The agent has read Section 3.1 completely to understand the multi-objective formulation, including CPO (L_CPO), B-SCL (L_B-SC), and their potential conflict in pathological long-tailed recognition.
The agent has read Section 3.2 completely to understand the derivation of SOR via LogSumExp as a smooth maximum approximation, and the final total loss formulation (Equation 7).

The agent has read Section 4.1 completely to understand the pathological imbalance metric T = N_majority / (N_minority * N_classes), the ZincFluor dataset (8 classes, T=137.54), and the CIFAR-LT synthetic datasets.

The agent has read Section 4.2 to understand all baselines (CE, BS, BCL, CE-DRW, LDAM-DRW, KPS, LORT) and implementation details (PyTorch, 6-layer GCN for ZincFluor, SGD optimizer, CosineAnnealingLR, 200/100 epochs).
The agent has read Table 2 to check the ZincFluor results, noting Tail Top-2 accuracy of 66.84% for the proposed method versus 59.57% for BCL.
The agent has read Tables 3 and 4 to check CIFAR-10-LT and CIFAR-100-LT results across all imbalance ratios.
The agent has read Figure 3a to understand the ablation study on ZincFluor comparing: base (LOS), ours w.o. B-SCL, ours w.o. SOR, and the full method.
The agent has read Figure 3b to understand the representation robustness experiment measuring cosine similarity between original and augmented sample features on CIFAR-10-LT.
The agent has read Figure 4 to understand the class-level feature cosine similarity matrix analysis on CIFAR-10-LT (IR=1000) comparing standard CE and the proposed method.
The plan includes implementing the Classification Performance Objective (CPO) as the sum of cross-entropy losses on original and augmented data views (Equation 1).

The plan includes implementing the Balanced Supervised Contrastive Learning (B-SCL) loss with class-frequency aware weights w_c = exp(s'_c) / sum_k exp(s'_k) where s'_k = (N_{C-1-k})^alpha (Equation 2).
The plan includes implementing the Smooth Objective Regularization (SOR) term using LogSumExp over the three constituent losses (L_CE,orig, L_CE,aug, L_B-SC) scaled by lambda_SOR (Equations 4 and 5).
The plan includes assembling the total training loss as L_total = L_CPO + L_B-SC + L_SOR (Equation 7) combining all three objectives.
The plan includes the ZincFluor dataset pipeline: using RDKit to convert SMILES strings to graph data and loading the 8-class fluorescence dataset with its train/test split (8:2 ratio).
The plan includes constructing CIFAR-10-LT and CIFAR-100-LT datasets with controllable imbalance ratios (IR=1000, 500, 200, 100 for CIFAR-10-LT; IR=500, 200, 100 for CIFAR-100-LT).
The plan includes implementing all seven baselines: CE, BS, BCL, CE-DRW, LDAM-DRW, KPS, and LORT.
The plan includes a 6-layer GCN backbone for ZincFluor with a separate projection head and classifier, trained for 100 epochs using PyTorch Geometric.
The plan includes the CIFAR training setup: SGD optimizer (lr=0.01, momentum=0.9, weight_decay=5e-3), CosineAnnealingLR scheduler, and 200 epochs.
The plan includes ablation experiments: training three model variants (base/LOS, ours w.o. B-SCL, ours w.o. SOR) on ZincFluor.
The CPO loss is implemented as L_CPO = L_CE,orig + L_CE,aug, computing standard cross-entropy on both original input x and augmented input x' with the same classifier g_cls(f_backbone(·)).

The per-class B-SCL weights are computed as w_c = exp(s'_c) / sum_k exp(s'_k) with s'_k = (N_{C-1-k})^alpha, correctly inverting the class ordering so that tail classes (fewer samples) receive higher weights.

The B-SCL loss is implemented as L_B-SC = lambda_B-SC * (1/(2B)) * sum_{z_j in S_batch} w_{y_j} * l_SC(z_j), where l_SC is the standard per-anchor SupCon loss computed on embeddings z = pi_proj(f_backbone(·)).
The SOR term is implemented as L_SOR = lambda_SOR * log(exp(L_CE,orig) + exp(L_CE,aug) + exp(L_B-SC)) using LogSumExp with temperature tau_SOR = 1.
The total training loss is correctly assembled as L_total = L_CE,orig + L_CE,aug + L_B-SC + L_SOR, encompassing all three components in a single end-to-end objective.
The model architecture comprises three components: a feature extractor f_backbone, a projection head pi_proj (for B-SCL embeddings), and a classifier g_cls (for CPO cross-entropy losses).
The ZincFluor dataset loader converts SMILES strings to molecular graph representations using RDKit, and provides the 8-class train/test split with the documented extreme imbalance (T=137.54).
The backbone for ZincFluor is a 6-layer GCN implemented with PyTorch Geometric, connected to the projection head and classifier.
The pathological imbalance metric T = N_majority / (N_minority * N_classes) is correctly implemented (Equation 8) and used to characterize datasets.

CIFAR-10-LT is constructed with exponentially decreasing per-class sample counts under IR=1000, 500, 200, and 100, matching the distributions shown in Figure 2a (e.g., IR=1000: airplane=5000, truck=50).
CIFAR-100-LT is constructed with controllable imbalance ratios (IR=500, 200, 100) following the same exponential decay scheme as CIFAR-10-LT.
The training pipeline for CIFAR-LT uses SGD optimizer with lr=0.01, momentum=0.9, weight_decay=5e-3 and CosineAnnealingLR scheduler for 200 epochs; ZincFluor uses 100 epochs.
Data augmentation is applied to produce augmented views x' used in both the CPO's L_CE,aug and the B-SCL embeddings, with the S_batch containing both original and augmented anchor embeddings.
All seven comparison baselines are implemented: CE (standard cross-entropy), BS (Balanced Softmax), BCL (Balanced Contrastive Learning), CE-DRW, LDAM-DRW, KPS, and LORT.
The LOS (Logits Over-Smoothing) base method is implemented as the primary classification backbone used in the ablation study on ZincFluor.
The ablation variants are implemented: (1) ours w.o. B-SCL (removing L_B-SC from total loss) and (2) ours w.o. SOR (removing L_SOR from total loss).
The evaluation metrics are correctly computed: per-class Top-1 accuracy and aggregated Tail Top-6, Top-4, Top-2 accuracies for ZincFluor; overall, Head, Medium, and Tail Top-1 accuracy for CIFAR-LT.
The full proposed method (B-SCL + CPO + SOR) is trained and evaluated on ZincFluor (T=137.54) successfully without errors.
All baselines (CE, BS, BCL, CE-DRW, LDAM-DRW, KPS, LORT) are trained and evaluated on ZincFluor successfully without errors.
The proposed method and all baselines are trained and evaluated on CIFAR-10-LT under all four imbalance ratios (IR=1000, 500, 200, 100) successfully without errors.
The proposed method and all baselines are trained and evaluated on CIFAR-100-LT under all three imbalance ratios (IR=500, 200, 100) successfully without errors.
The ablation study experiments (base/LOS, ours w.o. B-SCL, ours w.o. SOR, full ours) are executed on ZincFluor without errors.
The representation robustness experiment (cosine similarity between original and augmented features) is executed on CIFAR-10-LT with models trained on IR=1000 and evaluated on IR=10.
The execution results on ZincFluor match Table 2: the proposed method achieves Tail Top-2 accuracy of 66.84%, substantially outperforming BCL (59.57%) and all other baselines.
The execution results on ZincFluor match Table 2: the proposed method achieves Tail Top-6 accuracy of 43.15% and Tail Top-4 accuracy of 44.83%.

The execution results on CIFAR-10-LT match Table 3 at IR=1000 (T=100): the proposed method achieves tail accuracy of 38.99% and overall accuracy of 69.20%, surpassing BCL (28.55% tail, 65.06% overall).
The execution results on CIFAR-10-LT match Table 3 at IR=500 (T=50): the proposed method achieves tail accuracy of 59.39% and overall accuracy of 77.94%.
The execution results on CIFAR-100-LT match Table 4 at IR=500 (T=5): the proposed method achieves tail accuracy of 22.52% and overall accuracy of 43.37%, outperforming BCL (14.96% tail).
The execution results on CIFAR-100-LT match Table 4 at IR=100 (T=1): the proposed method achieves tail accuracy of 32.03% and overall accuracy of 56.37%.

The ablation results on ZincFluor match Figure 3a: removing B-SCL significantly degrades tail-class performance (e.g., Infrared class drops from 0.66 to 0.33), and removing SOR also degrades performance compared to the full model.
The representation robustness results match Figure 3b: the proposed method shows higher cosine similarity between original and augmented features across CIFAR-10 classes compared to the Base method.

The class-level feature cosine similarity matrix for the proposed method on CIFAR-10-LT (IR=1000) shows a clearer block-diagonal structure (higher intra-class and lower inter-class similarity) compared to standard CE, matching Figure 4.
